# Leank — Instant Secure P2P Communication Platform
> https://leank.space

## What is Leank?
Leank is a free, open, browser-based Peer-to-Peer (P2P) communication platform. It enables instant video calls, voice chat, screen sharing, file sharing, text messaging, and shared YouTube watch sessions — all without signups, downloads, or backend servers.

## Core Value Proposition
- **Zero accounts required** — start communicating in seconds
- **Peer-to-peer architecture** — data flows directly between users, never through a central server
- **End-to-end privacy** — no recordings, no logs, no data retention
- **Browser-native** — works in any modern browser on any device

## Key Features
1. **Video & Voice Calls** — High-quality WebRTC-based P2P video and audio communication
2. **Instant Messaging** — Real-time text chat with no message history stored on servers
3. **Fast File Transfer** — Share files of any size directly between peers with no upload limits
4. **Screen Sharing** — Share your screen with connected peers in real time
5. **Shared YouTube Workspace** — Watch YouTube videos together with synchronized playback
6. **QR Code Room Sharing** — Instantly share room links via QR codes

## How It Works
1. A user creates a room — generating a unique, secure room ID
2. The room link or ID is shared with peers
3. Peers join the room and a direct WebRTC connection is established
4. All communication (video, audio, chat, files) flows P2P — no intermediary servers

## Technology Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js (React) with App Router
- **P2P Layer**: WebRTC via Trystero + simple-peer
- **Signaling**: Torrent-based signaling (no centralized signaling server)
- **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4
- **Icons**: Lucide React
- **Design**: Neo-brutalist, high-contrast, black-and-white UI

## Use Cases
- Quick private conversations without creating accounts
- Sharing sensitive files without uploading to cloud services
- Remote collaboration with screen sharing
- Watch parties with synchronized YouTube playback
- Anonymous communication in temporary rooms

## Privacy & Security
- No user accounts or personal data collection
- No backend database — zero data persistence
- P2P connections via WebRTC with DTLS encryption
- STUN servers used only for NAT traversal (connection handshake), never for relaying content
- Rooms are ephemeral — destroyed when all participants leave
